mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand that drives corporate earnings and equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of RSAFS growth are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in UNRATE below 4.5% indicates a tight labor market that compresses profit margins via wage pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months when UNRATE is below 4.5, or the negative predictive signal does not exceed a Sharpe ratio of 0.3 out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds the trailing 12-month average of CPIAUCSL inflation, the real policy rate becomes restrictive and depresses equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in the top quartile of real rate restrictiveness, or the conditional negative mean return does not differ from zero with statistical significance (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO over 3 months warns of an industrial recession that reduces corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than half of instances following a 3-month INDPRO decline, or the negative signal does not predict a lower mean return than the unconditional mean at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts (HOUST) combined with declining retail sales (RSAFS) signals a consumer-led demand contraction that depresses earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when both proxies decline, or the average negative return is not significantly different from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) above its 12-month moving average signals strong aggregate demand that boosts corporate profits and equity prices, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months when PAYEMS exceeds its MA, or the hit rate is below 55% out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: A flattening or inverted yield curve, proxied by FEDFUNDS exceeding a 12-month trailing average of itself, signals rising recession risk and tightening credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds its own 12-month MA by 75bp or more
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of months when the trigger condition holds, or the signal's information coefficient is below -0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 grows at a rate below its 2-year backward-looking average while HOUST declines, the economy exhibits weakening demand momentum that reduces forward earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2yr avg AND HOUST month-over-month decline
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of quarters when both conditions hold, or the conditional mean return is not significantly negative at the 10% level.
